articles.full_text:
JK Schools Summer Vacation Begins June 1; Authorities Issue Heatwave Advisory
Jammu and Kashmir government has announced summer vacations for schools in the Jammu division starting June 1 due to intense heatwave conditions. Primary classes will begin break from June 1, while higher classes from June 8. Check full details here.
Jammu Division School Holiday: Due to the continuous extreme heatwave conditions in the area, the Jammu and Kashmir administration has announced summer holidays for schools in the Jammu Division's summer zone beginning on June 1, 2026, which is a huge relief for parents and students.
The summer vacation would be enforced gradually, per the Directorate of School Education Jammu's (DSEJ) official directive. Students in Classes 6 through 12 will start their summer vacation on June 8, 2026, while primary classes will take a vacation on June 1. The updated schedule shall be adhered to by all government and accredited private schools located within the summer zone.
Keeping in view the prevailing weather conditions, summer vacations for schools falling under Summer Zone of Jammu Division shall commence w.e.f. 01.06.2026 for Primary Classes and w.e.f. 08.06.2026 for students of Classes 6th to 12th.
— Sakina Itoo (@sakinaitoo) May 23, 2026
Wishing all students a safe and enjoyable…
The decision was made in response to the rising heat in Jammu and the neighboring regions, where mercury values have been regularly exceeding 40 to 45 degrees Celsius in a number of locations. Students, especially younger ones, find it challenging to securely attend regular sessions due to the severe weather.
In order to prepare for reopening operations, such as academic preparation, classroom readiness, and cleanliness drives, teaching personnel will return to schools on July 21, 2026, according to the decree. Additionally, schools have been directed to make sure that instructors are still accessible for online academic support and direction throughout the break.
In addition, several institutions will set up summer tutorials and remedial sessions to help kids learn over the break. To guarantee ongoing academic engagement, schools have been instructed to provide scholastic labor to children. Additionally, the Education Department has issued a warning that organizations or employees that disobey the rules will face severe consequences. With this news, Jammu Division school children can anticipate an early summer vacation designed to protect their health and safety during the current weather.
